title: Brugada Syndrome
chief_complaint: My brother died suddenly in his sleep at age 30. The autopsy was negative. I've had episodes where I wake up gasping for air at night. I'm scared the same thing will happen to me.

physical_exam.Cardiac: Regular rhythm, no murmurs, no rubs, no gallops. PMI normal.

hidden_data.ecg_baseline: Sinus rhythm 72 bpm. Type 1 Brugada pattern: Coved ST elevation ≥2mm in V1-V2 followed by T wave inversion. QRS 110ms. Normal QT interval.
hidden_data.ecg_flecainide: Provocative test with Flecainide IV: Type 1 Brugada pattern becomes more pronounced. No arrhythmias induced.
hidden_data.echo: Structurally normal heart. EF 65%. No valvular disease. Normal RV and LV dimensions.
hidden_data.ep_study: Programmed ventricular stimulation induces polymorphic VT degenerating to VF. Patient successfully defibrillated.
hidden_data.genetic_test: SCN5A mutation (loss-of-function) - pathogenic variant. Confirms Brugada Syndrome Type 1.

correct_diagnosis: Brugada Syndrome Type 1 (SCN5A Mutation Positive) with Family History of Sudden Cardiac Death and Spontaneous Type 1 ECG Pattern
key_learning_points: Brugada syndrome = autosomal dominant sodium channelopathy (SCN5A mutation in 20-30%) causing increased risk of sudden cardiac death from polymorphic VT/VF
Type 1 ECG pattern (diagnostic): Coved ST elevation ≥2mm in ≥1 right precordial lead (V1-V3) followed by T wave inversion. Can be spontaneous or drug-induced
Clinical presentation: Syncope, nocturnal agonal respiration, sudden cardiac death (often during sleep or rest). Male predominance (8:1)
Risk factors for SCD: Spontaneous Type 1 ECG + history of syncope or aborted SCD, family history of SCD, inducible VT on EP study

Treatment: ICD is the ONLY proven therapy to prevent sudden death in high-risk patients. Quinidine for medical management. Avoid triggers: Certain drugs (www.brugadadrugs.org), fever, alcohol, large meals

patient_responses.episodes: About once a month I wake up suddenly at night feeling like I can't breathe. It lasts a few seconds and then I'm fine. I thought it was just nightmares or sleep apnea.
patient_responses.brother: He was 30, completely healthy. Went to bed and never woke up. The autopsy said 'sudden unexplained death'. No one could tell us why. He had similar nighttime episodes.
patient_responses.symptoms: No palpitations, no chest pain, no fainting. I exercise regularly with no problems.
patient_responses.medications: None. I avoid drugs completely after what happened to my brother.

feedback.correct_message: Outstanding diagnosis! The spontaneous Type 1 Brugada ECG pattern with positive Flecainide challenge, family history of sudden unexplained death, and inducible VT on EP study confirms high-risk Brugada syndrome. ICD implantation is life-saving. Genetic counseling for family members is essential.
feedback.attending_note: Implant dual-chamber ICD urgently. Educate about fever management (aggressive antipyretics - fever unmasks Brugada pattern). Avoid medications on Brugada drug list. Quinidine 600-900mg daily may reduce ICD shocks if recurrent. Screen all first-degree relatives with ECG ± Flecainide challenge. Genetic testing for family members (SCN5A cascade screening). No competitive sports. Follow-up EP clinic q6 months.
